overview: This B/R/G Modern deck leverages cheap Goblin creatures alongside targeted discard and combat tricks to overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize. By combining rapid board presence with hand disruption, it aims to dictate the pace and close games quickly.
win_conditions: Overwhelm with pumped Goblin tokens and creatures
Deplete opponent's hand via Conspicuous Snoop and Boggart Trawler death triggers
Push lethal damage using Birthing Ritual combat tricks

keep_criteria: Hands with two or more cheap Goblins
Birthing Ritual paired with a creature

Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably fetchable or duals), at least one mana acceleration/ritual (Boggart Trawler, Birthing Ritual, or Goblin Lackey), and a turn-1 or turn-2 threat (Goblin Guide, Goblin Lackey, or Conspicuous Snoop).
Dual lands (Steam Vents, Bloodstained Mire, etc.) are essential for B/R/G fixing.
mulligan_criteria: High mana curve with no early threats
Lack of interaction or board presence
Hands missing both discard and pump effects
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early threats, or hands with only high-cost spells and no acceleration.

early_game: Deploy low-cost Goblins to establish immediate pressure
Play Conspicuous Snoop or Boggart Trawler to force early discards
Hold up mana for potential combat tricks
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Goblin Lackey or Goblin Guide.
If opening with Birthing Ritual, play it, tap for {R}, then sacrifice it to fetch a Goblin (like Lackey or Guide).
Turn 2: Accelerate with Boggart Trawler or Conspicuous Snoop to find more threats, then attack with everything.
Prioritize playing fetch lands to find duals for B/R/G color fixing.
mid_game: Use Birthing Ritual to pump attackers or block efficiently
Cycle through discard triggers to strip opponent's answers
Maintain board advantage while searching for finishers
Mana is your most valuable resource.
Never overextend into sweepers unless you have a clear path to lethal.
Use Conspicuous Snoop to dig for specific threats or rituals.
Manage graveyard carefully if facing discard/recursion hate, and prioritize keeping Birthing Ritual for late-game value or surprise acceleration.
Commit threats early to force interaction.
If the opponent has sweepers (like Supreme Verdict or Wrath of God), hold back a threat or two to trade into it.
late_game: Leverage accumulated board presence to close out the match
Use remaining discard effects to clear late-game threats
Push for lethal with maximized combat damage
Lethal is typically achieved through combat damage with a flooded board of 1-2 mana Goblins, Kuldotha Forgemaster triggers, or Goblin Bombardment.
Birthing Ritual and Conspicuous Snoop help dig for the final threats when the game extends past turn 4.
Go all-in when you have 3+ mana, at least two threats, and the opponent has no sweepers or removal in hand.
Also commit when you can close the game in 1-2 turns, even if it means leaving yourself vulnerable to a single-target removal spell.

key_cards.name: Birthing Ritual
key_cards.notes: Cast during combat to boost attackers or save blockers; synergizes with cheap Goblins to push lethal

key_cards.name: Conspicuous Snoop
key_cards.notes: Sacrifice or let it die to force opponent to discard, disrupting their game plan early

key_cards.name: Boggart Trawler
key_cards.notes: Provides early board presence and triggers discard on death, applying pressure while stripping resources

sequencing_tips: Prioritize playing discard creatures before pumping to maximize hand disruption
Save Birthing Ritual for combat tricks rather than static board states
Manage hand size carefully to avoid discarding key cards when multiple death triggers resolve
Common mistake: overcommitting into sweepers without checking for interaction.
Unique interaction: Birthing Ritual can be sacrificed to fetch a Goblin even if you're low on mana, enabling surprise turns.
Conspicuous Snoop can find lands if you're flooded, but prioritize Goblins.
B/R/G splash requires careful land management
Always play around the metagame's top combo decks by keeping early disruption in hand.

matchups.advice: Focus on stripping counterspells and removal via discard triggers, then push aggressive damage
Race the board while using discard to remove their key threats, prioritize blocking efficiently
Establish early pressure before they stabilize, use discard to delay their value engines

Use multiple low-cost threats to force counter trade-offs. Play around single counters with redundant threats. Use Birthing Ritual's one-time effect to bypass counters if needed, and save a threat for when they tap out for counterspells.
Apply pressure immediately. Don't let them stabilize. Use discard to hit their ramp spells or key creatures. If they outpace you, prioritize finding lethal damage over board development.

Win the resource war. Prioritize discard, track their hand, and use Rabblemaster synergy. Trade efficiently and don't overcommit into their sweepers. The player who manages their graveyard and hand disruption better will win.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (Jeskai Blink), sweepers (Boros Energy/Affinity), and combo win conditions (Ruby Storm, Amulet Titan).
Key tells include opponent tapping for multiple colors early, drawing extra cards, or playing artifact/creature synergies.
Adjust your aggression based on whether they have board wipes or single-target removal in hand.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Goblin archetype in Modern with Birthing Ritual, Conspicuous Snoop, Boggart Trawler

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards provide pump and discard effects

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Interaction between pump and discard mechanics in B/R/G Goblin lists

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Standard Modern threats relevant to aggressive discard strategies
